home.title: AI agent setup — in seconds
home.lead: AI Kit configures Claude Code and OpenCode for any project from a single manifest. Paste one prompt, answer a few questions, and the agent writes a manifest and runs the binary — no scripts, no template fetches, ~85+ rendered files.

what.title: Everything an AI agent needs to start working in your repo
what.sub: AI Kit produces top-level instructions, host configs, subagent definitions, slash commands, skills, an MCP servers file, planning scaffolds and a KnowledgeOS-compatible vault — all driven by .aikit/manifest.yaml. Works with Kotlin, Java, Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust and more.

providers.title: Two hosts
providers.body: Render for Claude Code, OpenCode, or both at once — agent bodies are shared via INCLUDE.

languages.title: Profile-driven
languages.body: Five orthogonal axes (language, framework, host, provider, capability) compose the kit cleanly.

planning.title: Planning + vault
planning.body: .planning/ scaffold plus a per-module KnowledgeOS vault (concepts/reference/how-to/tutorials/guidelines).

agents.title: Agent + command + skill set
agents.body: Pre-baked subagents, /kit-* slash commands, and skill specs — ready to dispatch.

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.body: The recommended way — paste a single prompt into Claude Code or OpenCode and let the agent do the rest.

cli.title: CLI Usage
cli.body: Prefer running things yourself? Author a manifest and call verify + generate.

files.title: Generated Files
files.body: See exactly what AI Kit writes into your repository and what each file does.

build.title: Build from Source
build.body: Compile the binary yourself with Gradle and Kotlin/Native.

quickstart.title: From zero to a configured project
quickstart.lead: The fastest way: paste a single prompt into Claude Code or OpenCode inside your project.
quickstart.step1: Open Claude Code or OpenCode in your project directory.
quickstart.step2: Paste the prompt below and let the agent take it from there.
quickstart.step3: The agent collects project facts, writes .aikit/manifest.yaml, downloads the binary, runs verify in a loop until the manifest is clean, and finishes with generate.
quickstart.tip: Re-running generate overwrites kit-managed files unconditionally. Back up customised CLAUDE.md or settings before re-running.

cli.title: CLI Usage
cli.lead: Prefer running the binary yourself? Download it from the Releases page, drop a manifest at .aikit/manifest.yaml, and call verify + generate.

verify.description: Parse and validate the manifest against the schema. Does not write anything.

generate.description: Validate the manifest, then render every kit file into the target. Overwrites existing files.

help.description: Print help text and exit. Plain text, for humans.

version.description: Print the binary version and exit.

exitCodes.invalid: Manifest invalid (verify) or generation refused due to invalid manifest.
exitCodes.error: Usage error or runtime failure (missing file, parse error, I/O failure).

cli.manifestNote: The manifest path is positional and optional — it defaults to .aikit/manifest.yaml relative to the current directory. JSON output is always single-line on stdout, so agents can pipe it through jq.

files.title: Generated Files
files.lead: What lands in your project after running generate. Single-host installs get ~85 files; multi-host (opencode + claude-code) ~135.

purposes.claudemd: Top-level guidance Claude Code reads on every session — also acts as the @Main orchestrator body.
purposes.agentsmd: Top-level guidance OpenCode (and other AGENTS.md-aware tools) reads on every session.
purposes.claudeSettings: Permissions, env vars, and hooks for Claude Code.
purposes.opencodeJson: OpenCode project configuration — provider, models, permissions, MCP, LSP, formatter.
purposes.mcpJson: Project-scope MCP servers for Claude Code (context7, knowledge-my-app, serena).
purposes.claudeAgents: Subagent definitions used by Claude Code, dispatched via the Agent tool.
purposes.opencodeAgents: Subagent definitions used by OpenCode, dispatched via the task tool.
purposes.skills: Skill specs that agents load on demand.
purposes.tasks: Per-task scaffold (TASK.md.template) the agent instantiates per work item.
purposes.vault: KnowledgeOS vault scaffolded per module: concepts/, reference/, how-to/, tutorials/, guidelines/.
purposes.moduleAgents: Per-module instruction file under each module's source root.
purposes.autoMemory: Append-only learnings file shared by every agent across sessions.

build.title: Build from Source
build.lead: If you want to compile the binary yourself, you'll need JDK 21. Linking is per-target — pick the one for your host.
build.templatesNote: All templates live under kit-setup/templates/. A Gradle codegen task bakes them into the binary on every build, so editing an agent body, command, or schema is just a normal file edit followed by a rebuild.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.lead: Quick answers to common questions about AI Kit.

items.a: Yes — unconditionally. Anything you've customised (CLAUDE.md, settings.json, agent files) must be backed up before re-running. The agent that drives the install is responsible for preserving prior state.

items.q: Can I author the manifest by hand?
items.a: Yes. Start from kit-setup/templates/manifest.example.yaml, drop it at .aikit/manifest.yaml, and run kit-setup verify until it's clean. The schema is published at kit-setup/templates/kit/manifest.schema.json.
opencode, claude-code, or both. Multi-host renders both trees side-by-side and shares agent bodies through _shared/ via INCLUDE.

items.q: Why does the binary refuse my manifest with api_key_literal_detected?
items.a: It looked at provider.api_key_env or mcp.context7.api_key_env and matched a real-key heuristic (sk-... / ghp_... / AKIA... / 32+ chars high entropy). Use the env-var name (e.g. ROUTERAI_OPENCODE) instead and export the value via your shell.
Open an issue on the GitHub repository — link in the footer.
